sarga.title: Dasaratha requests Vasishta to commence the sacrifice-- orders various people skilled in their profession to attend to the preparations--Vasishta asks Sumantra to invite kings from various countries--extends hospitality-- Dasaratha commences the sacrificial ceremony with his wives

shlokas.original:
पुन: प्राप्ते वसन्ते तु पूर्णस्संवत्सरोऽभवत्।
प्रसवार्थं गतो यष्टुं हयमेधेन वीर्यवान्।।1.13.1।।
shlokas.translation: After completion of one year when spring returned, the valiant king (Dasaratha) entered the sacrificial pavilion to worship celestial beings for begetting sons.

shlokas.original:
अभिवाद्य वसिष्ठं च न्यायत: परिपूज्य च।
अब्रवीत्प्रश्रितं वाक्यं प्रसवार्थं द्विजोत्तमम्।।1.13.2।।
shlokas.translation: After offering humble salutations and worshipping sage Vasishta in accordance with prescribed ordinance for begetting children, he obediently said:

shlokas.original:
यज्ञो मे क्रियतां ब्रह्मन्यथोक्तं मुनिपुङ्गव।
यथा न विघ्न: क्रियते यज्ञाङ्गेषु विधीयताम्।।1.13.3।।
shlokas.translation: "O foremost among ascetics O brahman, may the sacrifice be performed according to  tradition in such a manner that the sacrifice is performed unhindered.

shlokas.original:
भवान् स्निग्धस्सुहृन्मह्यं गुरुश्च परमो महान्।
ओढव्यो भवता चैव भारो यज्ञस्य चोद्यत:।।1.13.4।।
shlokas.translation: You are an affectionate friend and most reverential spiritual guide to me. The burden of performing the sacrifice which has since commenced shall be borne by you".

shlokas.original:
तथेति च स राजानमब्रवीद्द्विजसत्तमः।
करिष्ये सर्वमेवैतद्भवता यत्समर्थितम्।।1.13.5।।
shlokas.translation: The great among brahmins answered him saying "I shall do everything as proposed by you".

shlokas.original:
ततोऽब्रवीद्विजान्वृद्धान्यज्ञकर्मसु निष्ठितान्।
स्थापत्ये निष्ठितांश्चैव वृद्धान्परमधार्मिकान्।।1.13.6।।
कर्मान्तिकान् शिल्पकरान्वर्धकीन् खनकानपि।
गणकान्शिल्पिनश्चैव तथैव नटनर्तकान्।।1.13.7।। 
तथा शुचीन्शास्त्रविद: पुरुषान् सुबहुश्रुतान्।
यज्ञकर्म समीहन्तां भवन्तो राजशासनात्।।1.13.8।। 
इष्टका बहु साहस्राश्शीघ्रमानीयतामिति। 0
उपकार्या: क्रियन्तां च राज्ञां बहुगुणान्विता:।।1.13.9।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter Vasishta summoned those brahmins who are experienced in the sacrificerelated activities venerable and righteous architects skilled workers brick makers, carpenters, diggers, artisans, account keepers, dancers and actors, men versed in scriptures and honest, wellinformed people and addressed them saying, "In accordance with the king's command let the work of the sacrificial ceremony be inauguarated. Let thousands of bricks be brought. Let temporary residential buildings be erected with all the comforts to accommodate royal guests."

shlokas.original:
ब्राह्मणावसथाश्चैव कर्तव्याश्शतशश्शुभा:।
भक्ष्यान्नपानैर्बहुभिस्समुपेतास्सुनिष्ठिता:।।1.13.10।।

तथा पौरजनस्यापि कर्तव्या बहुविस्तरा:।
आवासा बहुभक्ष्या वै सर्वकामैरुपस्थिता:।।1.13.11।।
shlokas.translation: In the same manner let widely spread residential accommodation be furnished with many items of food and all kinds of entertainment be provided for people from other lands.

shlokas.original:
तथा जानपदस्यापि जनस्य बहुशोभनम्।
दातव्यमन्नं विधिवत्सत्कृत्य न तु लीलया।।1.13.12।।
shlokas.translation: In the same way people coming from rural areas also should be duly, not casualy treated with respect.

shlokas.original:
सर्वे वर्णा यथा पूजां प्राप्नुवन्ति सुसत्कृता:।
न चावज्ञा प्रयोक्तव्या कामक्रोधवशादपि।।1.13.13।।
shlokas.translation: People from all castes should be welltreated with respect. There should be no insult  to any one through lust on greed on anger.

shlokas.original:
यज्ञकर्मसु ये व्यग्रा: पुरुषाश्शिल्पिनस्तथा।
तेषामपि विशेषेण पूजा कार्या यथाक्रमम्।।1.13.14।।
ते च स्युस्सम्भृतास्सर्वे वसुभिर्भोजनेन च।
shlokas.translation: The people deeply immersed in performing sacrificerelated activities and sculptors be specially honoured according to their nature of work. All of them should be satisfied with money and food.

shlokas.original:
यथा सर्वं सुविहितं न किञ्चित्परिहीयते।।1.13.15।। 
तथा भवन्त: कुर्वन्तु प्रीतिस्निग्धेन चेतसा।
shlokas.translation: Every thing should be wellmanaged. In whatever manner you do, not even the smallest things be left out. Carry out the work with love and affection in your hearts".

shlokas.original:
ततस्सर्वे समागम्य वसिष्ठमिदमब्रुवन्।।1.13.16।।
यथोक्तं तत्सुविहितं न किञ्चित्परिहीयते।
यथोक्तं तत्करिष्यामो न किञ्चित्परिहास्यते।।1.13.17।।
shlokas.translation: Then all of them approached Vasishta and said, "everything has been wellarranged in accordance with your instruction. There is no deficiency. Everything will be done as instructed by you without leaving out anything."

shlokas.original:
ततस्सुमन्त्रमानीय वसिष्ठो वाक्यमब्रवीत्।
निमन्त्रयस्व नृपतीन्पृथिव्यां ये च धार्मिका:।।1.13.18।।
shlokas.translation: Then Vasishta, having summoned Sumantra said to him "Invite all the righteous kings on earth".

shlokas.original:
ब्राह्मणान्क्षत्रियान्वैश्याञ्छूद्रांश्चैव सहस्रश:।
समानयस्व सत्कृत्य सर्वदेशेषु मानवान्।।1.13.19।।
shlokas.translation: "Extend an honourable invitation to brahmins, kshatriyas, merchants and sudras in their thousands residing in all countries.

shlokas.original:
मिथिलाधिपतिं शूरं जनकं सत्यविक्रमम्।
निष्ठितं सर्वशास्त्रेषु तथा वेदेषु निष्ठितम्।।1.13.20।।
तमानय महाभागं स्वयमेव सुसत्कृतम्।
पूर्वसम्बन्धिनं ज्ञात्वा तत: पूर्वं ब्रवीमि ते।।1.13.21।।
shlokas.translation: You may personally go and bring with due honour king Janaka who is the distinguished king of Mithila, a warrior whose truth is his strength proficient in all scriptures and the Vedas. Since he is an old ally, I am telling you in advance.

shlokas.original:
तथा काशीपतिं स्निग्धं सततं प्रियवादिनम्।
वयस्यं राजसिंहस्य स्वयमेवानयस्व ह।।1.13.22।।
shlokas.translation: Similarly, you may personally escort king of Kasi who is always affectionate and sweettongued. He is a friend to our king who is a lion among rulers.

shlokas.original:
तथा केकयराजानं वृद्धं परमधार्मिकम्।
श्वशुरं राजसिंहस्य सपुत्रं त्वमिहानय।।1.13.23।।
shlokas.translation: Likewise bring here the aged and very righteous king of Kekaya along with his sons. He is fatherinlaw to this lion among kings (Dasaratha).

shlokas.original:
अङ्गेश्वरम् महाभागं रोमपादं सुसत्कृतम्।
वयस्यं राजसिंहस्य समानय यशस्विनम्।।1.13.24।।
shlokas.translation: Bring with honour king of Anga, Romapada, famous and prosperous. He is an intimate friend of the king (Dasaratha).

shlokas.original:
प्राचीनान्सिन्धु सौवीरान्सौराष्ट्रेयांश्च पार्थिवान्।
दाक्षिणात्यान्नरेन्द्रांश्च समस्तानानयस्व ह।।1.13.25।।
shlokas.translation: Bring kings from the countries of the eastern and southern regions, from Sindhu, Sauvera and Saurashtra৷৷

shlokas.original:
सन्ति स्निग्धाश्च ये चान्ये राजान: पृथिवीतले।
तानानय यथाक्षिप्रं सानुगान्सह बान्धवान्।।1.13.26।।
shlokas.translation: Bring all other kings of this earth, who are friendly with us. Along with their retinues, families and relatives".

shlokas.original:
वसिष्ठवाक्यं तच्छ्रुत्वा सुमन्त्रस्त्वरितस्तदा।
व्यादिशत्पुरुषांस्तत्र राज्ञामानयने शुभान्।।1.13.27।।
shlokas.translation: Having heard the words of Vasishta, Sumantra speedily despatched virtuous and auspicious emissaries to bring the kings.

shlokas.original:
स्वयमेव हि धर्मात्मा  प्रययौ मुनिशासनात्।
सुमन्त्रस्त्वरितो भूत्वा समानेतुं महीक्षित:।।1.13.28।।
shlokas.translation: In accordance with the ascetic's order, righteous Sumantra himself quickly set out to escort Janaka and other kings.

shlokas.original:
ते च कर्मान्तिकास्सर्वे वसिष्ठाय च धीमते।
सर्वं निवेदयन्ति स्म यज्ञे यदुपकल्पितम्।।1.13.29।।
shlokas.translation: All the workers reported to the wise sage, Vasishta about the tasks completed for the sacrifice.

shlokas.original:
तत:प्रीतो द्विजश्रेष्ठस्तान् सर्वानिदमब्रवीत् ।
अवज्ञया न दातव्यं कस्यचिल्लीलयाऽपि वा।।1.13.30।। 
अवज्ञया कृतं हन्याद्दातारं नात्र संशय:। 28
shlokas.translation: Then, well pleased, the best among brahmins (Vasishta) said to them all "Nobody should be offered any thing with disregard. Anything given with contempt undoubtedly  results in the destruction of the donor."

shlokas.original:
तत: कैश्चिदहोरात्रैरुपयाता महीक्षित:।।1.13.31।।
बहूनि रत्नान्यादाय राज्ञो दशरथस्य वै।
shlokas.translation: Meanwhile kings from several countries to travelling nights and days arrived bringing with them various kinds of (precious) gifts.

shlokas.original:
ततो वसिष्ठस्सुप्रीतो राजानमिदमब्रवीत्।।1.13.32।।
उपयाता नरव्याघ्र राजानस्तव शासनात्।
मयापि सत्कृता: सर्वे यथार्हं राजसत्तमा:।।1.13.33।।
shlokas.translation: Thereupon, wellpleased, Vasishta addressing Dasaratha said "O best among men, beacuse of your command, kings from various countries have arrived. I have extended due hospitality to those noble kings according to their station."

shlokas.original:
यज्ञीयं च कृतं राजन् पुरुषैस्सुसमाहितै:।
निर्यातु च भवान्यष्टुं यज्ञायतनमन्तिकात्।।1.13.34।।
shlokas.translation: "O king, persons with composed minds have made preparations for the yaga. Now be pleased to leave for sacrificial altar nearby to perform yaga.

shlokas.original:
सर्वकामैरुपहृतैरुपेतं च समन्तत:। 
द्रष्टुमर्हसि राजेन्द्र मनसेव विनिर्मितम्।।1.13.35।।
shlokas.translation: O foremost of kings, you ought to see the sacrificial ground full of sources of entertainment arranged everywhere, as though improvised by imagination".

shlokas.original:
तथा वसिष्ठवचनादृश्यशृङ्गस्य चोभयो:।
शुभे दिवसनक्षत्रे निर्यातो जगतीपति:।।1.13.36।।
shlokas.translation: In response to the words of Vasishta and Rsyasringa, the Lord of the earth (Dasaratha) entered the sacrificial pavilion on a favourable day presided over by an auspicious star.

shlokas.original:
ततो वसिष्ठप्रमुखास्सर्व एव द्विजोत्तमा:।
ऋश्यशृङ्गं पुरस्कृत्य यज्ञकर्मारभन् तदा।।1.13.37।।
यज्ञवाटगतास्सर्वे यथाशास्त्रं यथाविधि। 3
shlokas.translation: At that moment the best of brahmins led by Vasishta and headed by Rsyasringa, entered the sacrificial enclosure and began the sacrifice in accordance with traditions and scriptural stipulations.                      इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे त्रयोदशस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the thirteenth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
